slides.title: Why *plain water* isn't hydrating you.
slides.sub: If you're chugging water and still foggy and tired, you might be missing what makes it work.

slides.title: Water alone falls short
slides.body: Water needs *electrolytes* — sodium, potassium, magnesium — to actually reach your cells instead of passing through.

slides.title: Spot the shortfall
slides.body: Headaches, *afternoon brain fog*, and muscle cramps can all point to low electrolytes, not just low water.

slides.title: Add a pinch of salt
slides.body: A *pinch of sea salt* and some citrus in your water gives plain H2O what it's missing.

slides.title: Food counts
slides.body: Bananas, leafy greens, yogurt, and avocado are loaded with *potassium and magnesium*. Hydration is partly a plate.

slides.title: Balance, not buckets
slides.body: More water isn't always better. *Drinking to thirst* plus minerals beats forcing a gallon a day.

slides.title: Hydrate so it *actually counts*.
slides.body: Vibe Health tracks your water with sleep, food, and steps, so you can see hydration move your daily score.

caption:
Drinking tons of water but still tired and foggy? Water alone might not cut it. 💧

Electrolytes — sodium, potassium, magnesium — are what get water into your cells. Add a pinch of salt and eat your greens. Save this. 🔖

Vibe Health tracks your water with your sleep, food and steps in one score → link in bio.
youtube_title: Why Drinking More Water Isn't Fixing Your Energy
